title: 10 best herbal teas for gum pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with gum pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The soothing and calming effects of lemon balm also ease tension and stress, allowing for a more relaxed oral environment. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and inflammation, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the jaw and gums, reducing tension and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties can help to combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of dental problems.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, the leaves are excellent for reducing inflammation and soothing gum pain.
Flowers: Containing calming compounds like citral and geraniol, the flowers help to ease pain and discomfort associated with gum issues.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the lemon balm leaves for five minutes in the boiling water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste and stir well before consuming.
Enjoy the tea hot within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with gum pain because it contains menthol, a natural analgesic that numbs the affected area, providing instant relief from toothaches, gum recession, and sensitivity. The tea's cooling effect also reduces inflammation and swelling in the gums, promoting healthy blood flow and tissue healing. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat gingivitis and periodontal disease by reducing plaque buildup and killing bacteria that can cause pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol in peppermint tea helps numb the gums, reducing pain and inflammation, making it an effective natural analgesic.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontitis.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of peppermint tea can help combat bacterial infections that cause gum pain, such as dental caries, plaque, or abscesses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, peppermint roots help reduce swelling and ease gum pain.
Leaves: Packed with menthol and other oils, peppermint leaves provide a soothing and refreshing relief from gum discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or two teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it over the peppermint leaves in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for five minutes before straining the liquid with a spoon or tea infuser.
Add one teaspoon of honey if desired to sweeten the tea and help soothe gum pain.
Drink the tea hot immediately after preparation, holding it in your mouth for at least 30 seconds to allow the menthol to work on the gum pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with gum pain because its active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins, have anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. The tea's antimicrobial agents also help combat bacteria that can cause periodontal disease, a leading contributor to gum pain. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids work synergistically with its other compounds to promote healing and restore oral health, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea contains compounds like triterpenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat bacteria that can cause periodontal disease and gum infections, reducing the risk of tooth loss and promoting healing.

Analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties: The bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflower tea, including sesquiterpenes, have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe gum inflammation and prevent infections.
Seeds: Seeds contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in gum pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia root and two cups of water.
Boil the water in a saucepan for ten minutes before adding the dried roots.
Steep the roots in boiling water for five to seven minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly and drink it three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with gum pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's active ingredients, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to soothe the gums and mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and soreness. Additionally, licorice teas' sweet flavor can be a pleasant distraction from the pain, allowing individuals to relax and enjoy their tea while experiencing reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease or injuries, making it a natural remedy for soothing gum discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from further damage and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing gum pain.

Mucilage: The mucilage in licorice root tea forms a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated gum tissues, providing relief from discomfort, inflammation, and bleeding associated with gum disease or injuries.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids, which have antibacterial and antiviral effects, making it effective against gum infections and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in accordance with recommended botanical standards.
Measure two to three teaspoons of dried licorice root powder into a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil one cup of purified water and steep the licorice root mixture for 5-7 minutes to extract optimal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or strainer, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, ideally within 10-15 minute preparation time frame.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The antioxidants present in herbal teas, such as peppermint and chamomile, can also soothe the gums and alleviate inflammation caused by gum disease or trauma. Additionally, herbal teas like ginger and sage have been shown to relax the muscles and calm the mind, reducing overall stress and tension that may contribute to gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal teas, such as peppermint, chamomile, and ginger, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, thereby relieving gum pain.

Antioxidants: Herbal teas like green tea, white tea, and hibiscus are rich in antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to gum pain and other oral health issues.

Astringent properties: Herbs like sage, myrrh, and licorice root have astringent properties that can help reduce bleeding, shrink swollen tissues, and soothe irritated gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to alleviate toothache and gum pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, barks help reduce inflammation and promote healing of gums, making them effective for relieving gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves in two cups of water for five minutes.
Add one tablespoon of fresh turmeric root juice and let it steep for an additional three minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids.
Combine one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with the strained tea liquid and let it infuse for two minutes.
Serve the tea hot, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia teas helps with gum pain because of its unique composition that targets the root causes of discomfort. The tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, stevia's antibacterial properties combat the bacteria that contribute to gum disease, promoting healthy gums and reducing the risk of further pain and infection. By soothing and healing the gums, stevia teas provide long-term relief from chronic gum pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Antioxidants in stevia tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to gum pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stevia tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, luteolin, and kaempferol, which may help alleviate gum pain by reducing swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of stevia tea can help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain, such as periodontitis and gingivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation and pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce gum bleeding and soothe oral mucous membranes, providing relief from gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried stevia leaves from a trusted source or store-bought dried stevia flowers for 20g per serving.
Heat filtered water in a tea kettle to a temperature of 90-95°c and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Measure the cooled water at 250ml and combine with the dried stevia leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Store the strained tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and serve chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated gums. The active compound gingerol in ginger tea reduces inflammation and relieves pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, ginger's warming effect can help ease tension in the jaw and mouth, providing temporary relief from gum discomfort caused by tooth sensitivity, gingivitis, or oral surgery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from gum pain.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of ginger tea, particularly the presence of paradol, help numb the pain receptors in the gums, reducing discomfort and pain associated with gum disease or oral surgery.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum pain and disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving gum pain.

Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gum inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ginger roots measuring one teaspoon per cup of water.
Boil one cup of water in a pot then reduce heat to medium low.
Add the dried ginger roots to the boiling water and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. The tea's natural oils also have antibacterial properties that combat the underlying infection causing the pain. Additionally, sassafras contains antioxidants that help protect the gums from further damage, promoting healing and soothing irritated tissues. As a result, regular consumption of sassafras teas can provide relief from gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sassafras tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as safrole and lignans, can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in sassafras tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to pain and inflammation.

Astringent properties: Sassafras tea's astringent compounds, such as tannins, can help reduce bleeding and shrink swollen gum tissue, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with gum problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, the roots are used to make a tea that helps reduce inflammation and ease gum pain.
Barks: Containing a compound called safrole, the barks have been traditionally used to treat gum disease and reduce swelling.
Seeds: The seeds of the sassafras plant contain a natural analgesic that can help numb the gums and provide relief from pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried sassafras bark or root for every eight ounces of water needed.
Combine the sassafras herb with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to release into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the resulting tea immediately while still warm, three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm irritated gums. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by toothaches, gum disease, or dental procedures. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial properties help combat bacteria that can cause inflammation and infection, promoting a healthy mouth environment and providing relief from painful symptoms. By incorporating chamomile tea into your oral care routine, you can experience significant pain reduction and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains flavonoids, terpenes, and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from gum pain.
Antibacterial properties: Chamomile's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain, reducing the risk of further irritation and promoting healing.
Soothing apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid found in chamomile, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, chamomile roots help soothe and calm gum inflammation.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and luteolin, the flowers provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that aid in reducing gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers or purchase dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 100-gram quantities.
Rinse the dried flowers with warm water and soak them in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey to sweeten if desired and store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Drink two cups of tea per day, brewed fresh each time, at least 30 minutes before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea's compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause gum irritation. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids help to soothe and calm the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate oral health issues, making it a popular choice for those seeking an alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum inflammation and pain, reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, easing discomfort and pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontitis.
Analgesic properties: Wild marjoram contains analgesic compounds that can help numb the area affected by gum pain, providing temporary relief from soreness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate gum inflammation and reduce pain.
Barks: Containing tannins that help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, making it effective in treating bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh wild marjoram leaves measuring one teaspoon or about 2-3 grams for each cup of boiling water.
Rinse the harvested wild marjoram leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep one teaspoon of dried wild marjoram leaves in a teapot or infuser in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup removing any loose herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool and settle for 2-3 minutes before consumption.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum pain are a natural and soothing way to alleviate discomfort and inflammation caused by gum disease, toothaches, or dental work. These teas work by reducing swelling, easing pain, and fighting bacteria that can contribute to gum issues. Examples of herbal teas that help with gum pain include peppermint, chamomile, and ginger tea. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing relief from constant discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods and drinks again, and promoting overall oral health and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum pain with herbal teas is to brew a soothing cup of peppermint tea. Peppermint's cooling properties help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. Another option is sage tea, which has antibacterial properties that combat bad breath and gum infections. Drink the tea twice daily, hot or cold, as needed. Additionally, try pairing tea with a saltwater rinse to further soothe irritated gums.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as toothaches, mouth sores, and swollen gums. Herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and eucalyptus have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate discomfort and reduce swelling in the mouth and gums. These teas can also soothe sore throats, relax tense muscles, and promote overall oral health when consumed regularly as part of a healthy routine.
